title: pinus koraiensis subsp. prokoraiensis
intro: Pinus koraiensis subsp. prokoraiensis, also known as Korean red pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the Korean Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Korean red pine include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and enhancing immune function. In herbal preparations, the bark, needles, and seeds of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of ailments and promote overall well-being.
